nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lovetot,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:
from Anglo-Norman French louet ‘wolf-cub’, a diminutive of lou ‘wolf’. The nickname may also have been used as a personal name. Compare Love , Lovell , Low .
from the Middle English female personal name Luvot or Lovet, pet forms of Love .

variants.name: Lovette
meanings: English: altered form of   Lovett  .

variants.name: Lovitt
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Lovett  .

variants.name: Love
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name derived from the Old English female name Lufu ‘love’, or the masculine equivalent Lufa. Compare Leaf 2.

English and Scottish: nickname from Anglo-Norman French love ‘she-wolf’ or simply ‘wolf’, the word love being a back-formation from the diminutive forms lovel and lovet ‘little wolf’. See also Low 3, from Anglo-Norman French lou, the more usual form of the word.
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhionghuin (see McKinnon ).

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Worksop
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Warsop  .

variants.name: Warsop
meanings: English: habitational name from either of two places in Nottinghamshire: Warsop and Worksop. There was confusion between these two placenames from an early date.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William de Lovetot, Lord  Worksop was born in 1252, in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Mrs William de Lovetot.

fr.lifeSummary: William de Lovetot, Lord  Worksop est né en 1252, à Worksop, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs William de Lovetot.

de.lifeSummary: William de Lovetot, Lord  Worksop wurde 1252 in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Mrs William de Lovetot mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: William de Lovetot, Lord  Worksop è nato il nel 1252 nel Worksop, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs William de Lovetot.
